---
title: "`r params$report_title`"
author: "`r params$report_author`"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Engine flag: TRUE only when this template is stitched into the
  # combined report (set by the parent). Leave false for normal renders.
  combined_mode: false
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: !r here::here("1_data", "master_macro_panel.csv")
  
  # Variables
  entity_var: "district"
  time_var: "year"
  value_var: "literacy_rate"
  
  # Time Parameters (The two anchor points to compare)
  start_time: 2021
  end_time: 2025
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Literacy Rate (%)"

  # --- Report Text (all editable; leave "" to use the smart default) ---
  report_title: "Longitudinal Panel: Slopegraph"
  report_author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
  chart_title: ""
  chart_subtitle: ""
  x_label: ""
  y_label: ""
  source_note: "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = !isTRUE(params$combined_mode),
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  dev.args = list(bg = "#FAF9F6"),
  fig.path = paste0(here::here("4_output", "figures"), "/"),
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r cerp-style, echo=FALSE, results='asis', eval=!isTRUE(params$combined_mode)}
cat("<style>", readr::read_file(here::here("2_R", "cerp_style.css")), "</style>")
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
if (!requireNamespace("ggrepel", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("The 'ggrepel' package is required for this template. ",
       "Run renv::restore() from the project root, then re-render.",
       call. = FALSE)
}
library(ggrepel)

source(here::here("2_R", "2.5_helpers.R"), local = TRUE)

# Load data (headers/values whitespace-scrubbed) and validate YAML params
raw_data <- cerp_load(params$data_path)
cerp_validate(raw_data, params[grepl("_var$", names(params))])

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
# Define string parameters OUTSIDE the mutate chain to prevent vector duplication
start_str <- as.character(params$start_time)
end_str <- as.character(params$end_time)

plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs only for the specific variables required
  drop_na(!!sym(params$time_var), !!sym(params$entity_var), !!sym(params$value_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Type-Safe Filtering: Convert data to character to match our safe parameters
  mutate(Time_Raw = as.character(!!sym(params$time_var))) %>%
  filter(Time_Raw %in% c(start_str, end_str)) %>%
  
  # 3. Scrub and wrap Entity names
  mutate(
    Entity = str_wrap(str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$entity_var)))), width = 15),
    Value = as.numeric(!!sym(params$value_var)),
    # Lock factor levels so start time is ALWAYS on the left
    Time = factor(Time_Raw, levels = c(start_str, end_str))
  )

# 4. Isolate data for direct left and right text labeling to avoid using a messy Y-axis
left_labels <- plot_data %>% filter(Time == start_str)
right_labels <- plot_data %>% filter(Time == end_str)
```


```{r plot-slopegraph}
source(here::here("2_R", "2.0_setup_theme.R"), local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Time, y = Value, group = Entity)) +
  
  # Draw the connecting lines
  geom_line(color = cerp_cols["neutral"], linewidth = 1.2, alpha = 0.8) +
  
  # Draw the anchor points at start and end
  geom_point(color = cerp_cols["primary"], size = 3.5) +
  
  # Direct Left Labels: Name + Value, repelled vertically so entities that
  # start at the same value fan out instead of overprinting
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = left_labels,
    aes(label = paste0(Entity, "  ", Value)),
    direction = "y", hjust = 1, nudge_x = -0.15,
    fontface = "bold", size = 4, family = cerp_font_caption,
    color = cerp_cols[["text"]],
    box.padding = 0.15, point.padding = 0.3,
    segment.color = "#b7c9d3", segment.size = 0.3, seed = 26
  ) +

  # Direct Right Labels: Value + Name, so every line is identifiable at BOTH
  # ends without tracing it back across the chart
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = right_labels,
    aes(label = paste0(Value, "  ", Entity)),
    direction = "y", hjust = 0, nudge_x = 0.15,
    fontface = "bold", size = 4, family = cerp_font_caption,
    color = cerp_cols[["text"]],
    box.padding = 0.15, point.padding = 0.3,
    segment.color = "#b7c9d3", segment.size = 0.3, seed = 26
  ) +
  
  # Expand limits horizontally so the text does not get cut off by the edge of the image
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.35, 0.35))) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Strip the Y-axis entirely for maximum data-ink ratio
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    axis.line.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = if (nzchar(params$chart_title)) params$chart_title else (paste("Shift in", params$metric_name)),
    subtitle = if (nzchar(params$chart_subtitle)) params$chart_subtitle else (paste0("Tracking regional trajectories from ", params$start_time, " to ", params$end_time, ".")),
    x = if (nzchar(params$x_label)) params$x_label else (NULL),
    y = if (nzchar(params$y_label)) params$y_label else (NULL),
    caption = params$source_note
  )
```
